annotations.label: Madhava's arctangent power series

anchor.snippet: The first term is the product of the given sine and radius of the desired arc divided by the cosine of the arc

annotations.note: Real.hasSum_arctan states arctan x = ∑ (-1)^n x^(2n+1)/(2n+1) for |x|<1, exactly Madhava's arctangent series.

annotations.label: Gregory's series

anchor.snippet: which in Europe was conventionally called Gregory's series after James Gregory

annotations.note: Gregory's series is the same arctan power series formalized as Real.hasSum_arctan.

annotations.label: Madhava–Leibniz series for π

anchor.snippet: The text also contains Madhava's infinite series expansion of π which he obtained from the expansion of the arc-tangent function

annotations.note: Real.tendsto_sum_pi_div_four proves ∑ (-1)^i/(2i+1) → π/4, the Madhava–Leibniz series for π.

annotations.label: Approximations of π to 11 and 13 decimals

anchor.snippet: Jyeṣṭhadeva gave values of π as 3.14159265359, correct to 11 decimals

annotations.note: Real.pi_gt_d20/Real.pi_lt_d20 rigorously bound π to 20 decimal places, subsuming the 11- and 13-decimal approximations.

annotations.label: Rapidly converging series for π

anchor.snippet: obtain a rapidly converging series by transforming the original infinite series of π

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration formalizes Madhava's convergence-accelerated transformations of the π series.

annotations.label: Remainder term method for π

anchor.snippet: The other method was to add a remainder term to the original series of π

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of the remainder-term correction added to the π series.

annotations.label: Sine and cosine series expansions

anchor.snippet: Proofs for the expansion of the sine and cosine functions

annotations.note: Real.hasSum_sin and Real.hasSum_cos give the Maclaurin series expansions of sine and cosine.

annotations.label: Sum and difference formulae for sine and cosine

anchor.snippet: The sum and difference formulae for sine and cosine

annotations.note: Real.sin_add, Real.cos_add, Real.sin_sub, Real.cos_sub formalize the sum and difference identities.

annotations.label: Geometric derivations of series
annotations.note: This refers to a proof methodology rather than a stated theorem, and Mathlib has no corresponding declaration.

annotations.label: Kuttakaram: integer solutions of linear systems

anchor.snippet: Integer solutions of systems of linear equations (solved using a system known as kuttakaram )

annotations.note: Int.gcd_eq_gcd_ab gives Bézout coefficients (the kernel of the kuttaka algorithm), but Mathlib has no general integer-solver for systems of linear equations.

annotations.label: Taylor series statements

anchor.snippet: Statements of Taylor series for some functions

annotations.note: taylor_mean_remainder_lagrange formalizes Taylor's theorem with Lagrange remainder, generalizing the text's Taylor-series statements.
